75018.subject: Why This Workplace Assessment Is Valuable In The AI Era
75018.body:
Forbes Edge

РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Forbes


РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Forbes

    
    
    РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°Р№СӮРө СҶСҺ СҒСӮР°СӮСӮСҺ РҪР° LinkedIn, СүРҫРұ РҝСҖРёС”РҙРҪР°СӮРёСҒСҸ РҙРҫ РҫРұРіРҫРІРҫСҖРөРҪРҪСҸ
РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°СӮРё РҪР° LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/why-workplace-assessment-valuable-ai-era-forbes-magazine-0tinc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B378BbksuQ8y%2FOu26YQ5Rrg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3_scWBC5zrgYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqs59a2l~oo-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqs59a2l-oo
  
With artificial intelligence taking over more routine tasks, employers are placing greater value on skills that canвҖҷt be replicated by technology, including communication, emotional intelligence and relationship-building. ThatвҖҷs why, as ForbesвҖҷ careers staff writer, I took it upon myself to take the popular behavior and communications assessment, DISC, to better understand my own strengths, blind spots and communication patterns. The assessment, which measures your behavioral style across four dimensionsвҖ”dominance (D), influence (I), steadiness (S), and conscientiousness (C)вҖ”offered a revealing look at how I naturally approach conflict, decision-making and collaboration.В  But after taking the assessment and finding out that my overthinking tendencies landed me in the conscientiousness category, a larger question came to mind: Is a decades-old behavioral assessment such as DISC still relevant for helping professionals grow today? To find out, I spoke to leadership coaches and DISC facilitators about how they use the assessment with executives, managers and teams in the age of AI. Below are my top takeaways from that conversation.В  A DISC Assessment Is More Than A Personality Test: According to author and executive leadership coach David Langiulli, DISC differs from other popular assessments like Myers-Briggs because it focuses more on your behavioral patterns rather than just your personality. He explains that your вҖңpersonality is relatively fixed,вҖқ but your behavior can be adjusted depending on the situation youвҖҷre in and the people youвҖҷre with. A DISC assessment, he said, is like вҖңa mirror reflecting back at you your behavioral and communications tendencies,вҖқ and it shows you how your strengths and/or weaknesses could impact your interactions at work.В  It Teaches You How To Navigate Conflict And How Others Respond To Conflict: Executive coach and DISC facilitator Julie Chance said, вҖңA lot of times two team members will be in conflict. People with the D-style move pretty quickly. People with the C-style like to think things through and they like to have more information to make decisions on. Oftentimes theyвҖҷre at an impasse because the person with the C-style thinks the person with the D-style is being reckless. And the person with the D-style thinks the person with the C-style is just making things difficult or stalling.вҖқ With the help of a DISC asses

75014.subject: Your AI Second Brain.
75014.body:
The Atlas

РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: GenAI Works


РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: GenAI Works

    
    
    РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°Р№СӮРө СҶСҺ СҒСӮР°СӮСӮСҺ РҪР° LinkedIn, СүРҫРұ РҝСҖРёС”РҙРҪР°СӮРёСҒСҸ РҙРҫ РҫРұРіРҫРІРҫСҖРөРҪРҪСҸ
РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°СӮРё РҪР° LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/your-ai-second-brain-genai-works-qlxve?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BmQBX9AuuRNWCbBYMix%2FMZA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2iLGaEnzzdgYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqs4wqzt~sm-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqs4wqzt-sm
  
Welcome back! This edition moves between useful ideas and uncomfortable questions. We start with something practical: how to build your own second brain using Claude and Obsidian. Then we look at ChatGPTвҖҷs biggest health-related upgrade so far, and what it could mean for people using AI to understand medical information. After that, we cover a new NIST finding that shows why AI guardrails may never be fully permanent. Finally, we end with a Google DeepMind system that matched physicians across multiple patient visits, raising a bigger question about how AI could support real healthcare over time. In today's Atlas Newsletter: How to build an AI second brain with Claude and Obsidian. Wire the two together into a knowledge system that compounds instead of resetting every session. Improving your health intelligence in ChatGPT. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 Instant reaches frontier-level health performance, free for everyone, with factuality errors down 71%. Your AI guardrails will be bypassed. A NIST proof extends GГ¶del's theorems to AI safety, and the takeaway reshapes how to secure agents. Towards conversational AI for disease management. DeepMind's AMIE matched primary care physicians over multiple visits, and out-scored them on precision and guideline alignment. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. How to build an AI second brain with Claude and Obsidian that gets smarter every day A new guide making the rounds shows how to turn Claude and Obsidian into a compounding knowledge system, so you stop starting every session from a blank chat window.В  It builds on Andrej Karpathy's LLM Wiki concept.В  Obsidian handles local storage and Claude acts as the brain reading and writing to it. How do these pieces fit together? вҶ’ The stack: Install Obsidian, a free local text store, and Claude Desktop Pro, the engine that reads and writes your files. вҶ’ The bridge: Add the Local REST API plugin inside Obsidian, copy the key, and connect the two through Claude's MCP framework with a single add command (claude mcp add-json obsidian-vault). вҶ’ The context: Ask Claude to interview you about your goals, projects and working style, then save its summary to a CLAUDE.md file. That file becomes the memory it loads every time, so it never forgets who you are. вҶ’ The structure: Create dedicated proj

75012.subject: Crypto miners shrug off selloff
75012.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”

==Hi all, happy Wednesday!В ==

==South Korea's markets fell 10% and crypto came off with it. BTC held best among the majors at -2.0%, roughly in line with gold, while perps (-6.2%), L2s (-6.1%), and DeFi (-5.9%) anchored the bottom. The only green on the board was equities вҖ” crypto miners ripped +4.5%, extending a trade that's now up ~73% on the year even as spot churns sideways.В ==

==Below that, we cover Synthetix retiring sUSD вҖ” a fixed 4-SNX conversion that hands holders a locked, diluted claim rather than their dollar back.==

вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

After the 10% decline in the South Korean markets, risk came off across the board, sorted cleanly by beta. BTC held up best among majors at -2.0%, roughly in line with gold (-2.1%) and inside the NASDAQ's -2.8%, but losses steepened the further out the risk curve you looked: Perps (-6.2%), L2 (-6.1%), and DeFi (-5.9%) anchored the bottom. The only green on the board came from equities. Crypto miners ripped +4.5% while the broader crypto equity cohort held flat-to-positive, a notable divergence from spot tokens.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/010f5f6a-0aa4-4f8f-8b64-81d1efe68bbc/image.png?t=1782307556)
Caption: 

Crypto miners have been the standout trade of 2026, and the YTD chart shows why yesterday's +4.5% pop was just a continuation. The index sits up roughly +73% on the year after a volatile journey to get there: a +30% January run, a full round-trip back to flat through February and March (bottoming near -20% at the end of Q1), then a near-vertical re-rating that began in mid-April and carried the cohort to a +88% peak in late May. Even after fading off those highs, miners remain the highest-beta expression of the AI-and-energy narrative bleeding into crypto and the only sector still printing triple-digit-adjacent gains while spot tokens churn sideways.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/83bea9e3-c350-49d4-8c94-871e505f6eef/image.png?t=1782307556)
Caption: 

Under the hood, that headline index return masks a dispersion of nearly 170 points between the top and bottom components, and the split is almost entirely a function of who has converted idle power capacity into signed hyperscaler contracts. The leaders have effectively stopped being miners and now trade as power-and-compute landlords, valued on
